topic: Section 4.1 — Part 4.1 post-ISS-1110 patch: Group C count 5 -> 6 standard-model imports (body + TLDR)
change_summary: Decider self-applied ISS-1110 in commit 242f6dc (2026-04-18 04:30 local): changed SEC-4.1 TLDR phrase "five globe/standard-model imports" to "six import globe / standard-model values" and Bottom Line paragraph "5 import standard-model or globe-model values" to "6 import standard-model or globe-model values". No other SEC-4.1 text changed; Group C table still has its 6 rows (K1 Tidal, S2 Solar Tidal, M2 Tidal, Equatorial Gravity, Aetheric Redshift Scale, P-wave Shadow Zone). Block length unchanged at 18,978 chars (c3 measured 18,959 chars; the 19-char delta matches the two "5" -> "6" character-level changes plus minor whitespace). Queue item 163 explicitly notes EXP-178 for phantom-WIN clarification (ISS-1109) is still pending and must NOT be re-flagged.
our_argument_summary: The section audits the dome April-2026 dashboard expansion from 20 to 28 scored domains at claimed 100% / 9.2sigma. Core argument: 14 cells are lambda_g-dependent (share a single fitted constant), 14 are static re-references to prior WINs (pred==obs hardcoded literals), 6 import known constants (sidereal day, WGS84 gravity, LambdaCDM Hubble, P-wave zone, tidal harmonics), 2 are Selleri/Lorentz recastings, 3 are loose directional checks, roughly 1 discriminates. Now that the group-C count is internally consistent at 6, the TLDR, body paragraph, and Group C table all agree numerically. The ISS-1110 fix closes c3 hole #4 cleanly.

tldr_review.notes: TLDR now reads "...six import globe / standard-model values (sidereal day, WGS84 gravity, LambdaCDM Hubble constant, P-wave shadow zone), two are Selleri-vs-Lorentz framework recastings with no data content...". The number 6 matches the Group C table row count. The 4 example imports are illustrative, not exhaustive (S2 Solar Tidal and M2 Tidal are not named), which is acceptable at TLDR length. The TLDR accurately represents the body argument. No TLDR factual errors.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Section-level prose, not an arithmetic pipeline. The only quantitative claim touched by the patch is the Group C classification count (6 rows). The six-row count is mechanically correct by table enumeration.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Not applicable to our own prose. The patch is a word-level edit.

dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The ISS-1110 patch itself introduces no new discrepancies. Pre-existing issues flagged by c3 remain: (1) phantom WIN-073/075/076 IDs in Group B are pending EXP-178 (ISS-1109 assigned-analyst) and deliberately out-of-scope per queue-163 note; (2) group arithmetic still inconsistent (14+14 claimed as partitioning 28, plus 6+2+3=11 "remaining", total 39); (3) Equatorial Gravity appears in both Group A row 4 AND Group C row 4, and GPS Clock Offset (Lorentz) appears in both Group B row 28 AND Group D row 1 — the count "6 imports" as currently written does NOT disclose that Equatorial Gravity is double-classified.

patch_verification.issue_addressed: ISS-1110 — Group C count 5 -> 6 standard-model imports (TLDR + body).
patch_verification.old_text_tldr: ...five globe/standard-model imports, two framework recastings, same handful of fitted constants...
patch_verification.new_text_tldr: ...six import globe / standard-model values (sidereal day, WGS84 gravity, LambdaCDM Hubble constant, P-wave shadow zone), two are Selleri-vs-Lorentz framework recastings with no data content, and the remainder share the same handful of fitted constants.
patch_verification.old_text_bottomline: Of the remaining cells, 5 import standard-model or globe-model values, 2 are Selleri / Lorentz framework recastings, and 3 are loose directional checks.
patch_verification.new_text_bottomline: Of the remaining cells, 6 import standard-model or globe-model values, 2 are Selleri / Lorentz framework recastings, and 3 are loose directional checks.
patch_verification.notes: Group C now contains 6 rows (K1, S2, M2, Equatorial Gravity, Aetheric Redshift, P-wave Shadow Zone). TLDR says "six", body says "6", table has 6 rows — three-way numerical consistency achieved. The c3 review offered two clean fixes: (a) drop Equatorial Gravity from Group C and keep count at 5, or (b) change count to 6 AND add a "(also Group A #4)" cross-reference annotation. The decider chose option (b)-lite — count changed to 6 but without the annotation. This is acceptable but leaves the overlap implicit rather than explicit; see hole 1 below.

holes_found.description: The ISS-1110 patch achieves surface-level numerical consistency (6 vs 6 vs 6) but does not disclose that Equatorial Gravity is double-classified (Group A row 4 AND Group C row 4). A dome defender can still charge: "Your own audit counts Equatorial Gravity twice — once as a lambda_g-dependent domain in Group A and once as a WGS84 import in Group C. Which is it? If you are counting overlapping classifications, you cannot simultaneously claim Groups A and B partition the 28 dashboard cells." This is the same underlying double-count issue the c3 review flagged as a moderate hole; ISS-1110 adjusted the number but did not adjust the framing.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a one-phrase annotation to Group C row 4: change "Equatorial Gravity | 9.832204 = 9.8322 m/s² ..." to "Equatorial Gravity (also Group A #4, overlapping classification) | ...". Parallel annotation for Group D first row: "GPS Clock Offset (Lorentz) (also Group B #28, overlapping classification)". This is a small edit (two in-table phrases) that closes the overlap hole without reshuffling counts.

holes_found.description: Group-count arithmetic is still internally inconsistent after the patch. Text explicitly claims Group A (14) + Group B (14) = 28 dashboard cells. Then "Of the remaining cells, 6 import... 2 are Selleri/Lorentz... 3 are loose directional checks" — that is 11 additional cells, for a total of 39, not 28. Either the A+B partition claim is wrong, or the C/D/E groups are subsets (overlapping classifications), not "remaining". The c3 review flagged this as moderate; ISS-1110 did not address it; it survives the patch. A careful dome defender still has a "38-vs-28" talking point (now "39-vs-28" after 5->6).
holes_found.recommendation: Pick one framing. Cleanest option (preserves the strong main argument): replace "Of the remaining cells" with "Overlapping classifications within the 28" and adjust the Bottom Line to say "Of the 28, 6 are also standard-model imports (Group C), 2 are framework recastings (Group D), and 3 are loose checks (Group E); these overlap with Groups A and B rather than being additional cells." This also directly supports the "one ruler, many rooms" thesis.

holes_found.description: "One ruler" overstatement survives the patch. The Bottom Line still reads "The dome model\u2019s lambda_g = 8,619 km is the ruler". Group A row 2 (Tesla Longitudinal Freq) shared constant column reads "lambda_g, v_a" — v_a = pi*c/2 is an independent fitted constant (derived from Tesla\u2019s 0.08484 s measurement). So 13 of 14 Group A domains use lambda_g; 1 uses v_a. The "one ruler" headline is softer than the evidence supports. This hole was flagged in c3 as moderate; not in ISS-1110 scope; persists.
holes_found.recommendation: Unchanged from c3: rephrase the Bottom Line analogy to "a handful of fitted constants" instead of "one ruler". This is consistent with the earlier paragraph\u2019s "roughly 10 independent numerical knobs" framing. Not in ISS-1110 scope, but worth a follow-up issue if no EXP is already assigned.

holes_found.description: Tesla Longitudinal Frequency explanation (Group A row 2) still says "~1.57c, a known superluminal phase velocity in the Earth-ionosphere waveguide." WIN-001 uses "spherical cavity resonator" phrasing. The Earth-ionosphere waveguide (TM0 between conducting Earth and ionospheric D-layer) is a different physical model than the spherical cavity resonator (standing wave inside a conducting sphere). Both are standard-physics, but they have different dispersion relations. A defender with basic RF expertise will catch this terminology mismatch. Flagged in c3 as minor; not in ISS-1110 scope; persists.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the phrase with "the phase velocity pi*c/2 of the fundamental mode of a spherical cavity resonator with Earth\u2019s radius — a standard RF textbook result, not a dome-specific phenomenon" (matching WIN-001 terminology). One-sentence fix.

holes_found.description: formula_runtime.json "exactly six formulas (FORM-V51-001 through FORM-V51-006)" claim in the body paragraph is still unverified live in this review. The raw-text archives enumerate formulas narratively but do not list FORM-V51-00N identifiers. Next curmudgeon run or analyst Mode-4 could verify via WebFetch; low priority given methodology.json was verified live in the SEC-timestamp-error-tab work this week.
holes_found.recommendation: Low-priority — schedule a live fetch of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/formula_runtime.json the next time a curmudgeon is reviewing SEC-4.1 or SEC-2b.5 content. If exactly six formulas present, add "(verified YYYY-MM-DD)" footnote. If count differs, file an issue.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome\u2019s dashboard ships TWO different sigma values for the same aggregate (unchanged from c3 — still not in the section). The static HTML audit box on live.html states "9.2sigma, p=1.2e-20" while the dynamic status_history.json snapshot in the same document reports "CROSS-DOMAIN sigma: 14.26 across 19 domains (Fisher\u2019s method)". Two different sigma values for the same claim, in the same document, at the same time. Our section cites only 9.2sigma. Adding a single sentence surfacing the 14.26-vs-9.2 discrepancy turns an already-strong audit into a self-refuting one using the dome\u2019s own data files. Worth filing as EXP or a minor-edit follow-up.

The dome\u2019s own Schumann derivation gives a DIFFERENT H0 than the dashboard uses (unchanged from c3). raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 53: "f = c/(4H0) -> H0 = c/(4x7.83) = 9,572 km". Constants box (line 21): H0 = 8,537 km. The two values differ by 12%. Either the Schumann derivation is decorative (not actually producing H0), or H0 was fitted elsewhere and the Schumann formula is inconsistent. This would add a direct self-contradiction that makes the "fitted once" argument undeniable.

kernel_of_truth.description: The ISS-1110 patch is surgically correct. The specific issue it addressed — numeric mismatch between the TLDR/body count and the Group C table row count — is cleanly closed. All three locations now read "6" and match the six-row table. This is a meaningful quality improvement: a careful reader checking the numbers will no longer find the 5-vs-6 inconsistency that c3 flagged. The decider chose the higher-count option (6), which preserves the section\u2019s overall narrative that the dashboard adds many labels for few independent knobs. The fix is low-risk and introduces no new factual claims.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Not applicable — this is our own prose. Equivalent here: the patch is correct but narrowly scoped. Four of the five pre-existing c3 holes remain in the current text (phantom WINs pending EXP-178; group arithmetic 14+14+6+2+3=39 vs 28; "one ruler" vs multi-constant overstatement; Tesla waveguide/cavity terminology). The section will be stronger with (a) the EXP-178 phantom-WIN clarification, (b) a one-phrase overlap disclosure on Equatorial Gravity and GPS Clock Offset, (c) reframing C/D/E as overlapping subsets rather than "remaining cells". The ISS-1110 fix is a clean win on its own terms but does not close the broader classification-consistency gap.

text_fingerprint.verdict: ISS-1110 patch correctly applied; 4 pre-existing c3 holes persist (1 pending EXP-178, 3 out-of-scope for this patch); no new issues introduced by the fix

deeper_analysis:
Taking the other side of the argument (per user preference):

A sympathetic read of the decider\u2019s choice: ISS-1110 addressed exactly what the c3 review called out at minor severity — "5 vs 6" numeric inconsistency — and did so with a minimal two-token replacement that did not reshuffle classifications. Any larger reframing (overlapping classifications, group-arithmetic overhaul) was out of scope for a self-apply patch and would have required an EXP. The decider correctly filed ISS-1109 for the phantom-WIN issue (to EXP-178) and left the remaining c3 moderate holes for a follow-up cycle. This is disciplined patch-hygiene: one issue, one fix, no scope creep.

An adversarial read: the TLDR-body-table three-way consistency is now superficially clean, but the UNDERLYING issue that the c3 minor hole pointed at — "Equatorial Gravity is arguably double-counted since it is also Group A row 4" — is exactly the overlap problem that drives the separate moderate hole on group arithmetic. By choosing "change count to 6" rather than "drop Equatorial Gravity from Group C", the decider made the display number match the table rows but DID NOT resolve the classification overlap. A dome defender reviewing the current section can still charge: "Your Group C includes Equatorial Gravity, but Equatorial Gravity is also in Group A. Your 14+14=28 + your 6+2+3=11 sums to 39, not 28. You fixed the label but not the bookkeeping." Adding a single annotation to the two overlap rows closes that attack; not doing so leaves it open.

Both reads agree that the ISS-1110 patch is correct on its own terms. They disagree on whether the resolution was sufficient. I lean toward filing a NEW moderate issue ("annotate overlapping classifications in Groups C and D") rather than treating ISS-1110 as a full close. The c3 review listed four recommended edits; only one has been applied.

Patch verification details: text-level diff matches the commit message. Block length 18,978 (was 18,959; delta +19 chars roughly matches "five"/"5" -> "six"/"6" and example-list expansion in the TLDR). No other SEC-4.1 fields changed. Git log confirms commit 242f6dc as the sole source of the post-c3 change to the Part 4 block. Three-way consistency (TLDR says six, body says 6, Group C table has 6 rows) is achieved.

Pending remediation (not in this patch, not re-flagged): (i) EXP-178 (ISS-1109) — phantom WIN-073/075/076 IDs; (ii) recommended analyst/decider follow-up for overlap annotation and group-arithmetic reframing; (iii) unverified formula_runtime.json six-formula count. The section is currently publishable; two or three small edits will make it bulletproof.

EXP-179 (ISS for SEC-2b.5 parameter-knob recount 10->12 + 2.8x factor) is not relevant to SEC-4.1 but may propagate upstream — the SEC-4.1 body paragraph still uses "ten independent numerical knobs". If EXP-179 concludes the correct count is twelve, SEC-4.1 must be re-patched to match. I flag this as a dependency, not a current hole: the number "ten" is consistent with the EXP-176 source language and does not become wrong until an EXP-179 outcome says so.
